summary: A 26,014-line concatenation of research dialogues, Lean sketches, source audits and speculative proposals. Stable mathematical claims were extracted into eight graph nodes and three research moves; speculative or status-dependent material remains quarantined.

promoted.title: Cluster expansion with holes and two support roles
promoted.rationale: This is the most stable repeated dependency: connected Mayer covers, active-skeleton incompatibility, full-target locality, ultralocal integration and a second Ursell expansion.

promoted.title: Weighted Ward defect generated by the variation of the weight
promoted.rationale: The identity follows algebraically from an exact unweighted Ward identity and a stated Leibniz rule; physical use remains a separate instantiation problem.

promoted.title: Gaussian covariance decay to a collar factorization defect
promoted.rationale: The source repeatedly separates exact independence from an interpolation defect and identifies a finite-dimensional theorem ladder that is mathematically reusable.

promoted.title: Coercivity from a block Poincare estimate and an adjoint mass term
promoted.rationale: Once the block Poincare hypothesis and adjoint energy identity are stated, the lower bound is an elementary finite-dimensional inequality.

promoted.title: Exact conversion between lattice and physical exponents
promoted.rationale: The definitions d_phys = a n and m_phys = m_lat / a yield an exact exponent identity; the source also correctly separates this from the unresolved continuum limit.

promoted.title: Constructive QFT and renormalization group as explicit domains
promoted.rationale: The source consistently relies on these two domains, which were implicit but absent from the canonical graph.

promoted.title: Concrete uniform one-step Yang-Mills RG activity problem
promoted.rationale: The source repeatedly distinguishes a defined gauge fluctuation activity from an opaque predicate carrying the desired estimate, yielding a precise project problem.

quarantined.title: Uniform supersymmetric decoupling to pure Yang-Mills
quarantined.rationale: No exact auxiliary representation or uniform polymer-profile decoupling theorem is supplied; the proposal can change the target theory and normalization.

quarantined.title: Localized nonperturbative Nicolai map
quarantined.rationale: The source contains an interface and potential payoff, but no construction, locality proof, inverse or pure-measure identity.

quarantined.title: Periodic-table symmetry and closed super-shell cancellation
quarantined.rationale: The representation-theoretic degeneracy is real, but its transfer to a background-covariant RG shell pairing and improved polymer bound is only an analogy.

quarantined.title: Petz recovery as a wormhole or material portal
quarantined.rationale: Recovery maps reconstruct states or observables under hypotheses; they do not provide matter transport, spacetime geometry or a stress-energy solution.

quarantined.title: Projective carrying through polymer trees
quarantined.rationale: The proposed mechanism lacks a defined operator, norm, contraction theorem and verified comparison to the existing positive tree majorant.

quarantined.title: Hodge-Mayer-Ward theory for lattice RG
quarantined.rationale: The elementary Ward identity is promoted separately, but the proposed contraction, cohomology split and nonlinear Mayer transgression are not established on the actual activity space.

discarded.reason: The document repeats large technical handoffs, theorem ladders and source requests with status-dependent variations that should not become canonical knowledge.
examples: The complete technical handoff beginning near line 6476 is repeated near line 7951.
Several mutually incompatible recommendations alternate between Appendix F, Gaussian collars and SUSY work.

discarded.reason: Large Lean files and patch transcripts refer to THE-ERIKSSON-PROGRAMME rather than this repository; only their reusable mathematical declarations were extracted.
examples: The AppendixFFiniteCover Lean snapshot beginning near line 9436.
Sandbox download links and patch-delivery notes.

discarded.reason: Publication rankings and novelty scores are useful conversation context but are not stable mathematical nodes, edges or proofs.
examples: Numerical scores assigned to speculative mechanisms.

discarded.reason: Claims about which commit, theorem or dependency is currently open can become false after every repository update and must be regenerated from the actual codebase.
examples: Statements that a specific finite compiler step is or is not already closed.

discarded.reason: The raw file contains 121 URLs and mixed secondary references; exact theorem numbers and constants are not promoted until checked against primary sources.
examples: Wikipedia links used for metaphorical material.
Equation-number claims copied across multiple audits.

verification_queue.request: Check the complete Appendix-F theorem, metric convention, active-support definitions, factorization step and exponent losses against the primary Dimock text.
verification_queue.reason: The raw file contains repeated but not independently verified equation-number and constant claims.

verification_queue.request: Transcribe the exact one-step gauge identity, scalar products, projectors, Hessian, localized propagator and raw activity norm from the relevant Balaban papers.
verification_queue.reason: These are the source-dependent inputs for the concrete Yang-Mills activity problem.

verification_queue.request: Determine whether the localized fluctuation measure is a literal product measure at the stage where exact target-family integration factorization is used.
verification_queue.reason: Exponential covariance decay alone does not justify exact independence.

verification_queue.request: Keep raw H, integrated K-sharp, renormalized H-sharp and scalar scale remainders as distinct typed objects in every source adapter.
verification_queue.reason: The raw file repeatedly warns that identically shaped exponential bounds can refer to different semantic activities.
